data.title: Salesforce (CRM): Solid AI Traction and Improving Pipeline Support a Favorable Risk/Reward Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Derrick Wood from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Salesforce and keeping the price target at $325.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Derrick Wood has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that suggest Salesforce is fundamentally well positioned despite some mixed signals. His checks point to a solid ramp in Agentforce and continued strength in Data Cloud, supporting a view that artificial intelligence offerings are gaining meaningful traction and should drive attractive AI-related annual recurring revenue in the near term.

At the same time, he expects organic subscription revenue growth to remain steady rather than re-accelerate sharply, which tempers the magnitude of the upside narrative but not the overall investment case. With the stock trading at what he views as a reasonable multiple of projected free cash flow and partner feedback indicating gradually improving deal activity and pipeline metrics, he believes the risk/reward skew remains favorable, justifying a constructive Buy stance on CRM.

In another report released yesterday, TipRanks – OpenAI also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $216.00 price target.

data.title: Snowflake: Strengthening Growth, Expanding AI Capabilities, and Reinforced Buy Thesis
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Derrick Wood has reiterated their bullish stance on SNOW stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Derrick Wood has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including evidence that Snowflake is set up for a stronger fourth quarter with re-accelerating product growth. His channel checks with major partners indicate demand running ahead of internal targets, supported by expanding data workloads, accelerating migrations, and early but growing adoption of Snowflake’s AI and analytics capabilities.

He also highlights improving consumption trends, rising AI-related annual recurring revenue, and the benefits of new offerings such as Snowflake Intelligence, Snowpark, Cortex, and Iceberg that are broadening use cases and user types. Coupled with a deepening partnership with hyperscalers, emerging vertical solutions, and confidence that management can at least meet or modestly exceed consensus growth expectations, Wood views Snowflake as a leading AI data platform and maintains it as a top pick.

data.title: Workday: AI-Driven Growth and Strategic Acquisitions Underscore Buy Rating Despite Macro and Leadership Headwinds
data.description:
Derrick Wood, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on Workday. The associated price target was lowered to $200.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Derrick Wood has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Workday’s consistent ability to deliver in-line results and its solid third-quarter performance with healthy subscription and cRPO growth. While recent checks for the fourth quarter and 2026 budgets were mixed and highlighted some macro and competitive headwinds, he still expects fiscal 2027 guidance and near‑term execution to remain broadly on track.

He also sees meaningful strategic value in Workday’s expanding AI and acquired product portfolio, such as HiredScore, Paradox, Evisort, and Sana, which are already contributing to ARR and should support future growth. Although the CEO transition introduces some optical disruption, Wood views Aneel Bhusri’s return as a medium‑term positive for Workday’s AI roadmap, and at roughly 11x EV to projected 2026 free cash flow, he believes the current valuation already discounts many of the AI and macro concerns, justifying a continued Buy stance.

In another report released on February 18, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $255.00 price target.

data.title: Superior Plus: Lowered Growth Targets and Deferred Catalysts Justify Market Perform (Hold) Rating
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, John Gibson CFA from BMO Capital downgraded Superior Plus to a Hold, with a price target of C$8.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

John Gibson CFA has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Superior Plus’s evolving outlook and capital allocation. While fourth-quarter results generally met expectations with modest EBITDA growth, segment performance was mixed, as strength in U.S. propane was offset by weaker results at Certarus driven by softer wellsite pricing.

More importantly, management materially lowered its medium-term EBITDA and free cash flow growth targets, reflecting weaker CNG pricing and certain one-time propane costs, and also scaled back its share repurchase plans. With key strategic initiatives now pushed out further and fewer near-term catalysts, Gibson reduced his target price and concluded that the risk‑reward profile is now balanced, supporting a Market Perform (Hold) stance.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating on Charles River Labs: Attractive Entry Point as AI Fears Overhang Fundamentals and Multiple Expansion Potential
data.description:
Analyst Charles Rhyee from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Charles River Labs and decreased the price target to $235.00 from $251.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Charles Rhyee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Charles River Labs’ earnings trajectory and valuation. He highlights that, despite a softer first-quarter outlook, management’s guidance implies solid adjusted EPS growth for the remainder of 2026, and he views the assumptions for the DSA segment as cautious given an improving biotech funding landscape.

Rhyee also notes that concerns around AI have likely pressured the stock more than fundamentals justify, as management sees AI as a tool that should spur additional DSA activity rather than displace it. With the shares trading well below their historical earnings multiple and his analysis indicating room for multiple expansion as AI fears ease, he concludes that the current price offers an attractive entry point for investors.

In another report released yesterday, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $200.00 price target.

data.title: Brinsupri’s Breakout Growth, Robust Balance Sheet, and Upcoming Clinical Catalysts Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Ritu Baral has maintained their bullish stance on INSM stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ritu Baral has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied primarily to Brinsupri’s outperformance and outlook. Brinsupri’s 4Q25 revenue of roughly $145M, supported by about 9,000 newly reimbursed patients, substantially exceeded prior Street and buy-side expectations, and first-time FY26 guidance of at least $1B in sales aligns with and validates her bullish revenue trajectory.

Ritu Baral’s rating is based on the view that Brinsupri’s ramp meaningfully surpasses prior specialty respiratory product launches while Arikayce continues to modestly grow and outperform prior guidance. She also highlights a solid balance sheet of approximately $1.4B in cash to fund commercialization and pipeline development, and sees upcoming clinical catalysts, including the Phase 3 flNTM ENCORE readout and Phase 2 brenso HS data, as additional drivers of long-term value.

data.title: Balanced Outlook on PTC: Sephience-Driven Growth Offset by Duchenne Headwinds and Unmodeled Vatiquinone Upside Supports Hold Rating
data.description:
Joseph Thome, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Hold rating on PTC Therapeutics. The associated price target remains the same with $75.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Joseph Thome has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including solid current performance and balanced future risks. He views PTC’s 2026 revenue outlook of $700–800 million as credible, supported chiefly by the strong early uptake of Sephience, which has exceeded prior expectations and is expanding globally, yet he notes this strength is largely reflected in current forecasts.

At the same time, he highlights ongoing headwinds in the Duchenne muscular dystrophy franchise, with generic competition for Emflaza and regulatory setbacks for Translarna weighing on longer-term visibility. He also points out that while the vatiquinone program for FA has a clearer regulatory path after recent FDA interactions, it still requires an additional study and remains outside his base-case model, leading him to a more neutral stance overall.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Rimini Street: Litigation Overhang Removed but Modest Growth Keeps Shares Fairly Valued at Hold
data.description:
Rimini Street, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Derrick Wood from TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $4.25 price target.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Derrick Wood has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including Rimini Street’s solid fourth quarter, improving remaining performance obligations, and management’s confidence in sales momentum supported by plans to expand sales capacity and increase quotas. At the same time, overall revenue growth remains modest, with certain regions and product lines showing flat or negative trends, and customer metrics such as retention and net new clients reflecting only incremental progress rather than a clear inflection.

Rimini Street now operates without the prior litigation overhang and is pursuing growth initiatives like its partnership with ServiceNow and Agentic AI for ERP, which could support better performance in the second half and beyond. However, despite an undemanding valuation on a free cash flow basis, Wood views the shares as fairly valued until the company can demonstrate a more sustained and meaningful reacceleration of growth, leading him to maintain a Hold rating rather than move to a more aggressive stance.

data.title: Telix: Dual-Product PSMA Strategy and Cash-Generating Diagnostics Base Support Buy Rating and Multibillion-Dollar Therapeutics Upside
data.description:
Telix Pharmaceuticals, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Tara Bancroft from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a A$20.00 price target.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tara Bancroft has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Telix’s strong growth outlook and expanding diagnostic franchise. He highlights that management’s 2026 revenue guidance of $950–970MM implies high‑teens to low‑20s percent growth and already comes in about 3% above Street expectations, supported by robust momentum in precision diagnostics and a full year of RLS contribution.

He also points to Telix’s dual‑product PSMA PET strategy with Illuccix and Gozellix, which is designed to capture a wider set of payors through differentiated reimbursement structures, thereby sustaining roughly 25% annual growth in diagnostic sales. In Bancroft’s view, this cash‑generating diagnostics base can be reinvested into an advancing therapeutics pipeline targeting a multibillion‑dollar market opportunity, creating an attractive risk‑reward profile that underpins his Buy recommendation.

In another report released on February 8, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a A$34.00 price target.
